summary: Run the canonical ordinary AW executor loop through final-response admission until terminal custody is authorized.

writes.when: persist each admission and continuation slice
the caller-supplied executor performs work before emitting a final attempt

steps.description: Run the caller-supplied executor command and treat stdout as the model-authored final response attempt.
Evaluate the terminal outcome contract against the executor final response attempt.
Run the selected ordinary continuation operation when the final response is not authorized.
Re-enter the executor with continuation state until DELIVERED, qualified BLOCKED, or USER_PAUSED authorizes final custody.
Persist the local admission and resume slice.

guards: This is the canonical ordinary AW/autopilot execution boundary for model-authored finals.
Every executor stdout final attempt must pass through final-response admission before it can be exposed as terminal output.
Rejected CONTINUE attempts must keep custody with the agent and re-enter the executor with active objective and continuation state.
Executor mode is conservatively classified as mutation-capable, non-idempotent, network-allowed, and outside-repo-write-capable because the executor command is caller supplied.
Local admission slices are continuity checkpoints and must not be treated as closure evidence.
proof: autopilot route tests execute the generated CLI command across multiple ordinary executor slices
autopilot route tests prove a real repository-state transition between a rejected CONTINUE final and an accepted DELIVERED final
generated package freshness checks include the Python and TypeScript autopilot command surfaces
